headline: AT&T leaves rivals flat-footed as bankrupt carrier folds
summary: Dish DBS, the satellite TV and wireless subsidiary of EchoStar, filed for prepackaged Chapter 11 bankruptcy on June 30 in federal court in Houston. The filing ends months of speculation about the future of the industry's would-be fourth wireless carrier. More than 88% of Dish's bondholders ...

headline: Verizon (VZ) Launches Carrier Fraud Tool As SOVA Deal Expands Enterprise Reach
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ), AT&T, and T-Mobile introduced a network-based mobile number authentication solution aimed at reducing AI-driven identity theft in the US. The carrier collaboration targets rising telecom-related fraud by embedding verification directly at the network level across their mobile systems. Separately, Endeavor Managed Services completed the acquisition of the largest Verizon-exclusive enterprise agent, expanding Verizon’s reach in connectivity and lifecycle...

headline: 3 Absurdly Cheap Dividend Stocks to Buy With $1,000 in July

That is exactly where opportunity hides in July. headline: Is Tutor Perini (TPC) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
summary: Based on the average brokerage recommendation (ABR), Tutor Perini (TPC) should be added to one's portfolio. Wall Street analysts' overly optimistic recommendations cast doubt on the effectiveness of this highly sought-after metric. So, is the stock worth buying?

headline: Lunda Construction Awarded Roberts Street Railroad Bridge Replacement Project in Minnesota
summary: BLACK RIVER FALLS, Wisc., July 08, 2026--Tutor Perini Corporation (NYSE: TPC) (the "Company"), a leading civil, building and specialty construction company, announced today that its subsidiary, Lunda Construction, has been awarded the Roberts Street Railroad Bridge Replacement Project over the Mississippi River. The project is situated in St. Paul, Minnesota.

headline: Where Oversold Meets Undervalued: 3 Dividend and Growth Plays for Income Investors
summary: Some beaten-down stocks are cheap for a reason, but a select few sit at the rare crossroads of technical washout and genuine undervaluation. Three names across telecom, consumer staples, and software currently occupy that territory, and they are not equally suited for retirement income.

headline: Wells Fargo cautious on AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile as Starlink looms
summary: Investing.com -- Wells Fargo initiated coverage of the three major U.S. wireless carriers with a cautious sector outlook, flagging SpaceX's Starlink as a growing competitive threat to fixed wireless access and postpaid account growth across the industry.

headline: Aduna with Major U.S. Carriers Introduces Network-Based Authentication as an alternative to SMS Codes
summary: Aduna, a leading global provider of standardized network APIs, today announced a major leap forward in digital security designed to reduce fraud across the U.S. mobile ecosystem. In collaboration with AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon, Aduna is rolling out a network solution engineered to help protect millions of US mobile users from the rising tide of AI-driven identity theft.
